nodes.title: Character Description
widgets_values: a stocky green goblin holding a purple orb
Generate a single character only, centered in the frame on a plain white background. Full body visible, facing right. Detailed 32-bit pixel art style.

nodes.title: Animation Prompt
widgets_values:
{"2x2_walk":"Create a 4-frame pixel art walk cycle sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 4 frames in a 2x2 grid on white background. The character is walking to the right.

Top row (frames 1-2):
Frame 1 (top-left): Right leg forward, left leg back - stride position
Frame 2 (top-right): Legs close together, passing/crossing - transition

Bottom row (frames 3-4):
Frame 3 (bottom-left): Left leg forward, right leg back - opposite stride
Frame 4 (bottom-right): Legs close together, passing/crossing - transition back

Each frame shows a different phase of the walking motion. This creates a smooth looping walk cycle.

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","2x2_jump":"Create a 4-frame pixel art jump animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 4 frames in a 2x2 grid on white background. The character is jumping.

Top row (frames 1-2):
Frame 1 (top-left): Crouch/anticipation - character slightly crouched, knees bent, preparing to jump
Frame 2 (top-right): Rising - character in air, legs tucked up, arms up, ascending

Bottom row (frames 3-4):
Frame 3 (bottom-left): Apex/peak - character at highest point of jump, body stretched or tucked
Frame 4 (bottom-right): Landing - character landing, slight crouch to absorb impact

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","2x2_attack":"Create a 4-frame pixel art attack animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 4 frames in a 2x2 grid on white background. The character performs an attack that fits their design - sword slash, magic spell, punch, kick, or energy blast.

Top row (frames 1-2):
Frame 1 (top-left): Wind-up/anticipation - preparing to attack, pulling back weapon or gathering energy
Frame 2 (top-right): Attack in motion - the strike or spell being unleashed

Bottom row (frames 3-4):
Frame 3 (bottom-left): Impact/peak - maximum extension of attack
Frame 4 (bottom-right): Recovery - returning to ready stance

Make the attack visually dynamic and exciting.

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","2x2_idle":"Create a 4-frame pixel art idle/breathing animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 4 frames in a 2x2 grid on white background. The character is standing still with subtle idle animation.

Top row (frames 1-2):
Frame 1 (top-left): Neutral standing pose - relaxed stance
Frame 2 (top-right): Slight inhale - chest/body rises subtly

Bottom row (frames 3-4):
Frame 3 (bottom-left): Full breath - slight upward posture
Frame 4 (bottom-right): Exhale - returning to neutral, slight settle

Keep movements minimal - a gentle breathing/idle loop.

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","3x3_walk":"Create a 9-frame pixel art walk cycle sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 9 frames in a 3x3 grid (3 columns, 3 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Contact - right foot planted forward, left leg trailing
Frame 2: Down - weight compresses onto the right leg, body lowest
Frame 3: Passing - legs cross under the body, torso rising
Frame 4: Up - left leg swings forward, body at its highest
Frame 5: Contact - left foot planted forward, right leg trailing
Frame 6: Down - weight compresses onto the left leg, body lowest
Frame 7: Passing - legs cross under the body, torso rising
Frame 8: Up - right leg swings forward, body at its highest
Frame 9: Neutral standing pose - a clean reference frame

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","3x3_jump":"Create a 9-frame pixel art jump animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 9 frames in a 3x3 grid (3 columns, 3 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Idle stance - standing ready
Frame 2: Crouch - knees bent deep, arms back, anticipation
Frame 3: Launch - legs extending hard, body springing upward
Frame 4: Rise - airborne, legs tucking up, arms raised
Frame 5: Apex - highest point, body tucked or stretched
Frame 6: Fall - descending, legs extending toward the ground
Frame 7: Land - impact, knees bending to absorb
Frame 8: Recover - rising back to the idle stance
Frame 9: Neutral standing pose - a clean reference frame

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","3x3_attack":"Create a 9-frame pixel art attack animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 9 frames in a 3x3 grid (3 columns, 3 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Ready stance - weapon held ready
Frame 2: Wind-up - weapon pulled back, weight shifting to the back foot
Frame 3: Full wind-up - maximum coil, gathering power
Frame 4: Strike begins - weapon accelerating forward
Frame 5: Full extension - the strike at maximum reach, most dynamic frame
Frame 6: Follow-through - weapon continues past the target
Frame 7: Recovery - pulling the weapon back under control
Frame 8: Return - settling back into the ready stance
Frame 9: Neutral standing pose - a clean reference frame

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","3x3_idle":"Create a 9-frame pixel art idle/breathing animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 9 frames in a 3x3 grid (3 columns, 3 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Neutral standing pose, relaxed
Frame 2: Slight inhale begins - chest rises subtly
Frame 3: Inhale continues - shoulders lift a touch
Frame 4: Full breath - tallest, subtle arm sway
Frame 5: Hold - momentary stillness at the top
Frame 6: Exhale begins - chest lowering
Frame 7: Exhale continues - shoulders settle
Frame 8: Neutral again - ready to loop
Frame 9: Neutral standing pose - a clean reference frame

Keep movements SUBTLE - a gentle loop, not dramatic motion.

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","4x2_walk":"Create a 8-frame pixel art walk cycle sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 8 frames in a 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Contact - right foot planted forward, left leg trailing
Frame 2: Down - weight compresses onto the right leg, body lowest
Frame 3: Passing - legs cross under the body, torso rising
Frame 4: Up - left leg swings forward, body at its highest
Frame 5: Contact - left foot planted forward, right leg trailing
Frame 6: Down - weight compresses onto the left leg, body lowest
Frame 7: Passing - legs cross under the body, torso rising
Frame 8: Up - right leg swings forward, body at its highest

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","4x2_jump":"Create a 8-frame pixel art jump animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 8 frames in a 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Idle stance - standing ready
Frame 2: Crouch - knees bent deep, arms back, anticipation
Frame 3: Launch - legs extending hard, body springing upward
Frame 4: Rise - airborne, legs tucking up, arms raised
Frame 5: Apex - highest point, body tucked or stretched
Frame 6: Fall - descending, legs extending toward the ground
Frame 7: Land - impact, knees bending to absorb
Frame 8: Recover - rising back to the idle stance

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","4x2_attack":"Create a 8-frame pixel art attack animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 8 frames in a 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Ready stance - weapon held ready
Frame 2: Wind-up - weapon pulled back, weight shifting to the back foot
Frame 3: Full wind-up - maximum coil, gathering power
Frame 4: Strike begins - weapon accelerating forward
Frame 5: Full extension - the strike at maximum reach, most dynamic frame
Frame 6: Follow-through - weapon continues past the target
Frame 7: Recovery - pulling the weapon back under control
Frame 8: Return - settling back into the ready stance

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell.","4x2_idle":"Create a 8-frame pixel art idle/breathing animation sprite sheet of this character.

Arrange the 8 frames in a 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows) on a white background, reading order: left to right, then top to bottom.

Frame 1: Neutral standing pose, relaxed
Frame 2: Slight inhale begins - chest rises subtly
Frame 3: Inhale continues - shoulders lift a touch
Frame 4: Full breath - tallest, subtle arm sway
Frame 5: Hold - momentary stillness at the top
Frame 6: Exhale begins - chest lowering
Frame 7: Exhale continues - shoulders settle
Frame 8: Neutral again - ready to loop

Keep movements SUBTLE - a gentle loop, not dramatic motion.

Use detailed 32-bit pixel art style with proper shading and highlights. Same character design in all frames. Character facing right. Plain white background. Keep the character's feet planted on the same baseline in every cell, and keep the character the exact same size and position within each cell."}

subgraphs.name: Remove Background (BiRefNet)
subgraphs.description: Removes or replaces image backgrounds using BiRefNet segmentation and alpha compositing.
